evals.prompt: Analyze the contradiction between offline availability and access control for EPUB books in our system
evals.expected_output: The agent identifies the technical contradiction, classifies it, maps it to TRIZ principles, and outputs analysis to analysis/triz-core-YYYY-MM-DD.md. The contradiction should be documented with Improving, Worsens, Why, and TRIZ Principles sections.
assertions: The agent identifies offline availability vs access control as a contradiction
The agent classifies it as a technical contradiction
The agent maps it to relevant TRIZ principles (e.g., Segmentation, Condition Separation)
The output is written to analysis/ directory with date format

evals.prompt: We have a contradiction: improving EPUB parsing performance makes the security model more complex. Analyze this.
evals.expected_output: The agent identifies the technical contradiction (performance vs security/complexity), explains why this happens, verifies the reality of the assumption, and maps to applicable TRIZ principles.
assertions: The agent identifies the performance vs complexity/security contradiction
The agent explains causality (why improving performance worsens security)
The agent verifies the assumption is realistic
The agent lists relevant TRIZ principles

evals.prompt: What are the physical contradictions in our offline sync system where the sync state must be both consistent and available?
evals.expected_output: The agent identifies physical contradictions where the system must be in two opposite states simultaneously, classifies them appropriately, and maps to separation principles.
assertions: The agent identifies the consistency vs availability physical contradiction
The agent classifies it as a physical contradiction
The agent maps to separation principles (Time/Space/Condition)

evals.prompt: Before I start coding the permission system, run the TRIZ analysis skill
evals.expected_output: The agent runs the full TRIZ analysis workflow: defines system parameters, identifies contradictions, classifies them, maps to principles, and documents to analysis/ directory.
assertions: The agent runs the full analysis workflow
The agent identifies all system contradictions
The agent maps each to TRIZ principles
The agent outputs to analysis/ file

evals.prompt: I need to understand the contradictions in our annotation sync system where we want local-first but also real-time consistency
evals.expected_output: The agent identifies the core contradiction between local-first (autonomy/speed) and real-time consistency (coordination/sync), explains the trade-offs, and maps to TRIZ principles.
assertions: The agent identifies local-first vs real-time consistency contradiction
The agent explains the trade-off clearly
The agent maps to relevant TRIZ principles
